title: Add Invoice — request form with saved contacts
what: The VCC's Add Deposit button is now Add Invoice: a full request form (deposit #, amount, send-to, CC, 35-character description) that emails the details to billing@unbridledmedia.com and records the request. Send To autofills from every client contact the platform has ever seen, and new name/email pairs are saved automatically for future invoices. Who to CC is a contact search with an + Add New option — each address becomes a removable pill. The billing email is a configurable automation (Invoice Request to Billing) in the admin Automations dashboard.
where: ProFi → open a project → VCC → Client Deposits → + Add Invoice
how: Open the project's VCC tab and click + Add Invoice in the Client Deposits tile.
Fill Deposit # and Amount.
Start typing in Send To — Name: pick a contact to autofill their email, or type a new name and email (saved for next time).
In Who to CC, search contacts and click to add, or type a new email and hit + Add New — addresses stack as pills with ✕ to remove.
Add the invoice description (35 characters max) and submit — billing@unbridledmedia.com gets the request email and the deposit line appears with the invoice number clickable to review.
Track every request in Reports → Client Invoice Report → Requests view.

title: Invoice Request to Billing — email automation
what: Submitting + Add Invoice in the VCC fires a branded request email to accounting with the deposit #, amount, the invoice's send-to and CC, the 35-character description, and who asked. It's now a managed automation in the admin Automations dashboard: the From and To addresses are editable (default billing@unbridledmedia.com) and the email design can be previewed. There is deliberately no CC field on the automation — the invoice's CC list travels inside the email body for accounting to use on the formal invoice.
where: Hub → Admin → Automations ▸ → Invoice Request to Billing
how: Open the Hub as an admin and click Admin, then Automations ▸.
Find Invoice Request to Billing in the list.
Edit From or To if the request should route somewhere else, and Save.
Click Preview to see exactly what accounting receives.
Every + Add Invoice submission from any project's VCC then emails that address automatically (once SMTP is connected).

title: Client Outstanding Invoice Report
what: One report tracking client invoice requests. The default Unsent view lists every invoice that has been requested from a project's VCC but not yet sent — status pill, project, budget owner, deposit #, description, send-to and CC, amount, and who requested it when — with a running unsent total in the header. The All Requests view adds the full history with ✓ Sent / Unsent status on each.
how: Open Reports from the Hub and click Client Outstanding Invoice Report.
The Unsent view loads first: everything requested but not yet sent, newest first, with the unsent count and dollar total up top.
Switch to All Requests for the full history including sent invoices and their sent dates.
Click any row to jump to that project in ProFi.

title: Project Estimate PDF
what: A client-ready Project Estimate generated straight from the budget on the Unbridled template. It auto-fills the summary block (client, project title, total, today as the sent date, a valid-through date 30 days out, and who prepared it) and rolls every budget line into the template's four investment buckets: scripting/storyboarding plus the coordination fee land in Pre-Production/Creative; shoots, photography, virtual recording, and misc in Production; the post section in Post-Production; and every travel line in Travel. The bottom line always equals the budget's grand total. It opens as a print-ready page with the print dialog popped — save as PDF or print — with no browser headers or footers on the output.
where: ProFi → open a project → Client Budget → Project Estimate PDF
how: Open the project's budget in ProFi and click Client Budget (top right).
Choose Project Estimate PDF in the pop-out.
A print-ready estimate opens in a new tab with the print dialog ready — pick Save as PDF (or a printer).
Check the four bucket subtotals; the total matches the live budget, so update the budget and regenerate if numbers move.

title: Share Budget Options
what: One pop-out for every client-facing view of the budget, opened from the Share Budget Options button on the Finance page. It gathers the Budget Overview, three share views — Itemized with Cost (the full line-by-line budget), Itemized — Subtotals Only (every line item and quantity, with dollars only at section subtotal and total level), and Non-Itemized — Subtotals Only (rolled-up section subtotals, no line detail) — plus the Project Estimate PDF. The last-used share view is remembered, and picking one opens the shareable client page in a new tab.
where: ProFi → open a project's budget → Share Budget Options (top right)
how: Open the project's budget in ProFi and click Share Budget Options.
Pick how the client should see it: Budget Overview, Itemized with Cost, Itemized — Subtotals Only, or Non-Itemized — Subtotals Only.
The client page opens in a new tab — copy its link to send; the choice is saved as the budget's share mode.
Use Project Estimate PDF from the same pop-out for the print-ready estimate document.

title: Checklists — custom groups, items, and deletes
what: Each of the four Logistics checklists (Recap, Interviews/B-Roll, Commercial/Creative, What to Bring to Set) can now be shaped per project: add your own groups, add items into any group (built-in or custom), and delete any row with the ✕ at its right. Customizations save to your browser per project, just like the checkmarks, and the progress counts include them.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Logistics → Producer Checklist
how: Open a checklist from the row of pills at the top of the Producer Checklist tab.
Click + Add Group, type a name, and hit Add — the new section appears at the bottom.
Click + Add Item, pick which group it belongs to, type the item, and Add.
Delete any row — built-in or custom — with the ✕ at its right (with a confirm).
Assignments (the person icon) and checkmarks work the same on custom items.

title: Per-shoot Gear tab — Person Responsible + DIT drives
what: The shoot's Gear Management tab now leads with who owns the gear: the Person Responsible for Gear picker sits at the top, with a DIT — Drives tile directly beneath it for tagging hard drives to this shoot. + Tag Drive opens a searchable picker (name, tag, serial, or location); tagging a drive that's on another shoot warns before moving it, and returned drives go back to their home location. Everything feeds the Hard Drives report. Storage Location, Online Rentals, and Rental House info follow below.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Gear → Gear Management
how: Open the shoot's Gear tab — Person Responsible for Gear is the first tile; pick the crew member who owns gear for this shoot.
In DIT — Drives, click + Tag Drive and search by drive name, asset tag, serial, or location.
Click a drive to tag it to this shoot (a warning appears if it's currently on another shoot).
Click Return on a drive when it comes back — it shows in the office again on the Hard Drives report.

title: Talent call times feed the Schedule
what: Individual talent call times now live on the schedule instead of a column on the crew sheet. Each talent's per-day call time — set in their Add/Edit Talent form under Call Times by Day, now with a call location field beside each time — appears on that day's Schedule timeline as its own 'Talent Call — [name]' entry with a Talent tag, their role, and a 📍 location. The same call time and location show on the talent's shared call sheet under the day header.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Crew → talent's Edit form → Call Times by Day (shows on Logistics → Schedule)
how: Open the shoot's Crew tab and click Edit on a talent row (or + Add Talent).
Under Call Times by Day, set the time and type the call location for each shoot day they're needed.
Save — the Schedule timeline for that day now shows 'Talent Call — [name]' at the right slot with the location.
The talent's shared call sheet shows their call time and location in the day header.

title: Hard drive tracking + Hard Drives report
what: Every hard drive in the asset database (HDD category) now has a live location. Tag drives onto a shoot from the shoot's Gear tab DIT — Drives tile (or its gear dashboard); return them when they come home. The Hard Drives report is the roster: every drive with where it is right now — ✈ out on a shoot (with the shoot code and who tagged it, since when) or 🏠 home at its office location — filterable to All / On Shoots / In Office and searchable by name, tag, serial, or location.
where: Reports → Hard Drives (tagging happens on each shoot's Gear tab)
how: Open Reports → Hard Drives to see the full roster and each drive's current location.
Filter with the All / On Shoots / In Office pills or search by name, tag, serial, or location.
Click a row on an out drive to jump to the shoot holding it.
To tag or return a drive, use the DIT — Drives tile on a shoot's Gear tab: + Tag Drive to send it out, Return when it's back.

title: Gear request availability — reservations subtracted
what: The equipment picker in the gear request form now shows what's actually available for your dates, not just what exists. Each model's count is the assets marked available in Asset Management minus the quantity sourced 'Internal' on any other shoot whose dates overlap yours — shown as e.g. '6 of 10 available · 4 on other shoots' with the reserved note in yellow (red at zero). Counts refresh if you switch the project on the form; shoots without dates are treated conservatively.
where: Gear Report → + New Gear Request → any department's + Add Gear picker (also on a shoot's Gear Request tab)
how: Open a gear request and pick the project — the inventory counts key off that shoot's dates.
Open a department's + Add Gear picker: each model shows 'X of Y available' after subtracting internal reservations on overlapping shoots.
A yellow 'N on other shoots' note shows how many are spoken for; zero available turns red.
Set quantities as usual — the counts are informational, so you can still request more and sort sourcing on the Gear List.

title: Gear Report — the Gear Manager's home base
what: The Gear Report absorbed the old FreePro Gear Management page (that toggle is gone). One page now has it all: a shoot timeline (gantt) capped at five rows with vertical scroll and pinned date headers, and the full shoot table — Countdown, code, name, Person Responsible, dates, Travel, gear request status (greyed shoots haven't requested), request name, the green (!) needs-assignment flag, and the four gear-source columns with the printable internal check-out list. + New Gear Request and the Asset Management database live in the header. Rows open the shoot's gear dashboard; a green ✓ quick-views the submitted request.
how: Open Reports → Gear Report — the timeline shows upcoming shoots (scroll vertically for more than five, horizontally through dates).
Scan the table: greyed rows still need a gear request; the green (!) means gear awaits source assignment.
Click + New Gear Request to submit one, or Asset Management to open the equipment database.
Click a row for the shoot's gear dashboard, a green ✓ to quick-view its request, or the print icon for the internal check-out list.

title: Creative Assets (AvocadoPost)
what: A Creative Assets tab next to each project's Video Tracker: drag photos, motion graphics, or any files anywhere onto the panel (or multi-select via + Upload Asset, 20MB each). It's a list view — file name (click to download), size, uploader, and date — and every asset can be tagged to one or more videos from the tracker. Tags show as chips with ✕ to untag, a 'Tag new uploads to' selector pre-tags drops, and every tag or upload logs a note in that video's activity feed so its history shows what arrived and when.
where: AvocadoPost → open a project page → Creative Assets tab
how: Open the project page in AvocadoPost and click Creative Assets (next to Project Video Tracker).
Drag files onto the panel, or click + Upload Asset — optionally set 'Tag new uploads to' first so drops land pre-tagged.
Tag any asset to more videos with its + tag… dropdown; click a chip's ✕ to untag one video.
Each tag/upload writes a note into that video's activity; click a file name to download it.

title: Travel — Driving with schedule tiles
what: A Driving section on the Travel tab (below Rental Cars) for crew driving to a shoot. + Driver opens a form with the driver (crew dropdown), tap-to-tag passengers, required From and To cities with live map-validated autocomplete (type 'Columbia MO' or 'Columbia, Missouri' — both work; unmappable entries are flagged in red), a required departure date and time, car make/model, and notes. Saving computes an estimated drive time from the route and feeds two tiles onto the Schedule timeline: 🚗 Drive Departure at the departure time (with the ~drive-time badge, route, car, and passengers) and 🚗 Approx. Drive Arrival at departure-plus-drive-time.
where: FreePro → open a shoot → Logistics → Travel → Driving → + Driver
how: On the Travel tab, click + Driver under the Driving section.
Pick the driver, tap teammates to tag them as passengers.
Type the From and To cities and pick from the live suggestions (state codes or full names both match); set the departure date and time; add the car make/model.
Save — the drive shows on Travel with the estimated drive time, and the Schedule timeline gets Drive Departure and Approx. Drive Arrival tiles for the right day(s).

title: Music Resources — team music library with tags
what: A shared library of music links — licensing platforms, go-to tracks, playlists — that anyone on the team can add to. Links are grouped by tags (Recap, Corporate Talking Head, Documentary, Show Opener, and more), and tag chips under the search bar filter the list to one group. New tags can be added by anyone; duplicates are blocked automatically. A search bar covers names, tags, notes, and links.
how: Open Reports & Resources from the hub and click the Music Resources tile.
Click a tag chip (e.g. Recap) to filter to that group; click it again to show all.
Click + Add Resource, enter the title and link, pick a Tag from the dropdown (or choose + Add new tag…), and add optional notes.
Use + Add tag next to the chips to create a new tag for everyone — duplicate names are rejected.
Click any link title to open it in a new tab; ✕ removes an entry.

title: Video References — inspiration library with tags
what: A companion library for reference and inspiration videos — style frames, past work, and examples to point clients at. It shares the same tag list as Music Resources, so the groups (Recap, Documentary, Concert Sizzle, Training Videos, etc.) stay consistent across both libraries. Filter with the tag chips, search across everything, and add new tags as the catalog grows.
how: Open Reports & Resources from the hub and click the Video References tile.
Click + Add Resource, paste the video link, give it a title, and pick a Tag so it lands in the right group.
Click a tag chip to see only that group — handy when pulling references for a specific video type.
Add notes like what the reference is good for (pacing, framing, look) so teammates know why it's saved.
